title: Decode-shaped M1x8 score tile for the Llama7B attention frontier
hypothesis: A decode-shaped signed-int8 M1x8 score tile with a packed 256-bit result row will preserve exact Llama7B QK score semantics while materially reducing compute area and result-drain service relative to scaling the measured M16x8 operational GEMM tile.

direct_comparison.primary_question: How do scalar-drain and packed-row M1x8 score tiles change measured area, delay, and the precision-aligned Llama7B token frontier relative to the semantically mismatched M16x8 recost?
include: one signed query scalar by eight signed key scalars per accepted head-dimension beat
final accepted beat included exactly once
scalar result stream versus one-cycle packed 256-bit score row
identical Nangate45 floorplan and timing sweep for both result interfaces
exclude: do not treat M16x8 cross-row reuse as valid across decoder heads with different key matrices
do not promote vectorless OpenROAD power as token energy
score SRAM remains a FakeRAM physical proxy until signed-off SRAM views are available

required_evaluations.objective: Prove exact M1x8 signed-int8 QK score accumulation and packed-row stream behavior against the dot-product reference.

expected_result.reason: PPA and cluster composition must use the single-query score shape exercised by autoregressive Llama7B decode.

required_evaluations.objective: Measure scalar-drain and packed-row M1x8 score tiles under identical Nangate45 clocks and floorplans.

expected_result.reason: The packed interface removes eight scalar drain cycles but its 256-bit physical interface must be charged.

required_evaluations.objective: Replace the M16x8 dense-tile scaling in the Llama7B operational component frontier with measured decode-shaped score-tile area, timing, and result service.

expected_result.reason: The active Llama7B decision path must not scale an M16 tile as if all rows were useful during M1 decode.

required_evaluations.objective: Prove the composed M1x8 producer, explicit score requantizer, score SRAM, two-pass replay, value rendezvous, and iterative divider against the integer performance model.

expected_result.reason: Cluster PPA is ineligible until scaling, score storage, replay ordering, value rendezvous, division, and backpressure preserve the end-to-end integer contract.

required_evaluations.objective: Compose the selected exact M1x8 score tile with the real score-bank and two-pass stream interfaces before chip-level extrapolation.

expected_result.reason: Only a composed producer, score store, replay engine, and divider can close local routing and activity energy.

required_evaluations.objective: Recompute the Llama7B bound from the measured composed cluster and explicitly charge all value-dimension slices.

expected_result.reason: A full Llama7B head requires 16 eight-dimensional value commands at the measured cluster boundary.

remaining_abstractions_after_this_step: Per-command score multiplier and shift are explicit cluster inputs; deriving that metadata from dynamic Q/K quantization scales remains outside this cluster.
The two-pass value replay matrix remains tiled at eight value dimensions until outer value-dimension accumulation is composed.
The installed FakeRAM views have no SRAM GDS and remain physical proxies.
Chip-level NoC, clock/reset, power delivery, and external HBM/DRAM remain later boundaries.
